source.title: Michele Orrù, *Revisiting Keyed-Verification Anonymous Credentials*, IACR ePrint 2024/1552

paper.statement: µCMZ (Figure 1) is a keyed-verification anonymous credential system with extractability
paper.summary: μCMZ is a keyed-verification anonymous credential system: extractable and anonymous in the algebraic group model, with the stated advantage bounds.

paper.statement: µBBS (Figure 1) is a keyed-verification anonymous credential system with extractability
paper.summary: μBBS is a keyed-verification anonymous credential system: extractable and anonymous in the algebraic group model, with the stated advantage bounds.

paper.statement: Let IOP be a polynomial interactive oracle proofs. Let PCS be a designated-verifier
paper.summary: Compiling a polynomial interactive oracle proof with a designated-verifier polynomial commitment yields a designated-verifier fully-succinct SNARK without pairings.

paper.statement: Preliminaries
paper.summary: The Notation paragraph opening §3 (only): the random-oracle hashes H_p : {0,1}* → ℤ_p (Fiat–Shamir transcripts) and H_𝔾 : {0,1}* → 𝔾 (hash-to-curve). The numbered subsections §3.1–§3.4 are tracked as their own rows.

paper.statement: Cryptographic assumptions
paper.summary: The hardness assumptions over a prime-order group generator used throughout: discrete log and its gap, q-strong and 2-power variants, and q-DDHI.

paper.statement: An algebraic message authentication code (MAC) for n attributes over
paper.summary: Syntax of an algebraic message authentication code for n attributes over a prime-order group: the algorithms Setup, KeyGen, MAC, and Verify.

paper.statement: Unforgeability of an algebraic message authentication code MAC.
paper.summary: The unforgeability-under-chosen-message-and-verification (UF-CMVA) security game for an algebraic MAC.

paper.statement: Zero-knowledge arguments
paper.summary: The zero-knowledge argument interface (prover, verifier, simulator) and its simulation-extractability, as used by the credential presentation proofs.

paper.statement: One-more unforgeability game for an anonymous token scheme AT with non-interactive issuance.
paper.summary: The one-more unforgeability game for an anonymous token scheme with non-interactive issuance.

paper.statement: Anonymous Tokens
paper.summary: Syntax and security of anonymous token schemes with non-interactive issuance.

paper.statement: A credential predicate is an efficiently-computable function φ mapping some
paper.summary: A credential predicate: an efficiently-computable function on attributes that fixes what a presentation proves.

paper.statement: A keyed-verification credential system KVAC = (S, K, I, P) for predicate family
paper.summary: A keyed-verification credential system KVAC = (Setup, KeyGen, Issue, Prove) for a predicate family.

paper.label: Correctness
paper.statement: A keyed-verification credential system KVAC for a family of n ≤
paper.summary: Honestly issued credentials always produce accepting presentations for the predicates they satisfy.

paper.statement: A keyed-verification credential system KVAC for a family of n ≤ poly(λ) attributes,
paper.summary: Anonymity: issuance and presentation are simulatable without the secret attributes, so presentations are unlinkable across executions.

paper.statement: Extraction game for a keyed-verification credential system KVAC with extractors Ext.I and Ext.P,
paper.summary: The extractability game for a keyed-verification credential system, with attribute extractors Ext.I and Ext.P.

paper.statement: A keyed-verification credential system KVAC for a family of n ≤ poly(λ) attributes,
paper.summary: Extractability: an extractor recovers the certified attributes from any accepting presentation.
R_iu, the μCMZ issuance user-proof relation: knowledge of $(\vec{m}, s)$ with C' = Σᵢ mᵢ·Xᵢ + s·G and policy φ($\vec{m}$) satisfied.
R_is, the μCMZ issuance server-proof relation: knowledge of $(x_0, u)$ with X₀ = x₀·H, U' = u·G and V' = x₀·U' + u·C''.
R_p, the μCMZ presentation-proof relation: knowledge of $(r', \vec{r}, \vec{m})$ opening the commitments C⃗ over U' and Z = Σᵢ rᵢ·Xᵢ − r'·H, with policy φ($\vec{m}$) satisfied.

paper.statement: The keyed-verification credential system µCMZ. Boxed , the part that may be removed for
paper.summary: The μCMZ keyed-verification credential system construction (a variant of MAC_GGM); the boxed part is removable for the one-more unforgeable variant.

paper.statement: In the algebraic group model, µCMZ is an algebraic MAC for n = poly(λ) attributes
paper.summary: In the algebraic group model, μCMZ is an n-attribute algebraic MAC (UF-CMVA secure) under 3-DL (and DL), with advantage Adv^{3-dl} + Adv^{dl} + 3/p.

paper.statement: If ZKP is a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rcmz , then µCMZ is a keyed-verification
paper.summary: If ZKP proves the relation R ⊇ R_cmz, then μCMZ is an extractable keyed-verification credential.

paper.statement: If ZKP is a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rcmz.p ∪ Rcmz.is , then µCMZAT is an
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_cmz.p ∪ R_cmz.is, then the variant μCMZ_AT is a one-more unforgeable anonymous token.

paper.statement: In the algebraic group model, µCMZ is an algebraic MAC for n = 1 attributes in Zp
paper.summary: Base case of Theorem 5.1: in the algebraic group model, single-attribute μCMZ is an algebraic MAC over ℤ_p.

The AGM verification polynomial for μCMZ unforgeability at n = 1: a winning forgery would force this identity in the secret exponents (η, x₀, xᵣ, x₁, u₁…u_q), which Lemma 5.4 shows cannot hold over the polynomial ring, reducing to 3-DL.

paper.statement: µCMZ is an algebraic MAC for n = poly(λ) attributes in Zp with advantage:
paper.summary: Reduces n-attribute μCMZ security to the single-attribute case, giving its algebraic-MAC advantage over ℤ_p.

The partial evaluation ψ(χ) of the μCMZ verification polynomial in Lemma 5.4: collapsing the perfectly-hidden mask pairs onto one fresh variable χ leaves a nonzero polynomial of degree ≤ 3 that vanishes at the challenge's discrete logarithm, so finding its roots recovers that logarithm.

paper.statement: Item (i) happens with advantage Advgapdl
paper.summary: In the μCMZ unforgeability proof, the first forgery case is bounded by the gap discrete-log advantage.

paper.statement: Item (ii) happens with advantage at most Advufcmva
paper.summary: In the μCMZ unforgeability proof, the second forgery case is bounded by the single-attribute MAC's UF-CMVA advantage.

paper.statement: If ZKP a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rcmz , then µCMZ is anonymous for
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_cmz, then μCMZ is anonymous (issuance and presentation are simulatable).

paper.statement: If ZKP is a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rcmz , then µCMZ is extractable for
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_cmz, then μCMZ is extractable for the supported attribute family.

paper.statement: In the algebraic group model, µCMZAT is an anonymous token for n = poly(λ)
paper.summary: In the algebraic group model, μCMZ_AT is a one-more unforgeable anonymous token for n attributes.

paper.statement: Item (i) happens with probability at most q 1/p + Advdl
paper.summary: In the μCMZ_AT one-more unforgeability proof, case (i) occurs with probability at most q/p plus the discrete-log advantage.

paper.statement: Item (ii) happens with probability at most 1/p + Advdl
paper.summary: In the μCMZ_AT one-more unforgeability proof, case (ii) occurs with probability at most 1/p plus the discrete-log advantage.

paper.statement: Item (iii) happens with probability at most 3(1/p + Adv2-dl
paper.summary: In the μCMZ_AT one-more unforgeability proof, case (iii) occurs with probability at most 3(1/p plus the 2-discrete-log advantage).

paper.statement: The keyed-verification credential system µBBS. The variable φ denotes the arbitrary predicates
paper.summary: The μBBS keyed-verification credential system construction.

paper.statement: In the algebraic group model, µBBS is an algebraic MAC for n = poly(λ) attributes
paper.summary: In the algebraic group model, μBBS is an n-attribute algebraic MAC, with the stated advantage.

paper.statement: µBBS is an algebraic MAC for n = poly(λ) attributes in Zp with advantage:
paper.summary: Gives the algebraic-MAC advantage of n-attribute μBBS over ℤ_p.

paper.statement: If ZKP is a proof system for R ⊇ Rbbs , then µBBS is a keyed-verification extractable
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_bbs, then μBBS is an extractable keyed-verification credential.

paper.statement: If ZKP is a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rbbs.is ∪ Rbbs.p , them µBBSAT is an
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_bbs.is ∪ R_bbs.p, then the variant μBBS_AT is a one-more unforgeable anonymous token.

paper.statement: In the algebraic group model, µBBS is an algebraic MAC for n = 1 attributes in Zp
paper.summary: Base case of Theorem 6.1: in the algebraic group model, single-attribute μBBS is an algebraic MAC over ℤ_p.

paper.statement: µBBS is an algebraic MAC for n = poly(λ) attributes in Zp with advantage:
paper.summary: Reduces n-attribute μBBS security to the single-attribute case, giving its algebraic-MAC advantage over ℤ_p.

paper.statement: Let ZKP be a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rbbs , then µBBS is anonymous
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_bbs, then μBBS is anonymous.

paper.statement: If ZKP is a proof system for the relation R ⊇ Rbbs , then µBBS for n = poly(λ) is
paper.summary: If ZKP proves R ⊇ R_bbs, then n-attribute μBBS is extractable.

paper.statement: There exists a reduction for the game OMUFµBBSAT ,A (λ, n) to q- DLGrGen,A (λ) with:
paper.summary: Reduces the μBBS_AT one-more unforgeability game to q-strong discrete log (via Cheon's attack), quantifying the security loss.

paper.statement: There exists a p.p.t. adversary A for one-morenunforgeability of µBBSAT that can
paper.summary: Exhibits a p.p.t. adversary breaking μBBS_AT one-more unforgeability through the Lemma 6.12 reduction.

paper.statement: A designated-verifier polynomial commitment scheme (PCS) over the field
paper.summary: Syntax and security of a designated-verifier polynomial commitment scheme over a field.

paper.statement: dvKZG is a perfectly hiding polynomial commitment scheme.
paper.summary: dvKZG, a pairing-free designated-verifier variant of KZG, is a perfectly hiding polynomial commitment scheme.

paper.label: PIOP
paper.statement: A polynomial interactive oracle proof for an indexed relation R over
paper.section_title: IOP compiler for designated-verifier polynomial commitments
paper.summary: A multi-round interactive oracle proof whose prover messages are low-degree polynomials, for an indexed relation.

paper.statement: Let IOP be a knowledge-sound, polynomial interactive oracle proof over field family
paper.section_title: IOP compiler for designated-verifier polynomial commitments
paper.summary: Compiling a knowledge-sound PIOP with a designated-verifier polynomial commitment yields a knowledge-sound designated-verifier argument.

paper.statement: Let IOP be a q-query bounded zero-knowledge polynomial interactive oracle proof for
paper.section_title: IOP compiler for designated-verifier polynomial commitments
paper.summary: Compiling a bounded zero-knowledge PIOP with dvKZG yields a zero-knowledge designated-verifier argument.

paper.statement: Let IOP be a q-query bounded zero-knowledge polynomial interactive oracle proof
paper.section_title: IOP compiler for designated-verifier polynomial commitments
paper.summary: Combining the soundness and zero-knowledge compilers gives a designated-verifier fully-succinct zk-SNARK without pairings.

paper.statement: Let ` > 0, and Φ ⊃ {φprf , φrange }. If KVAC be a keyed-verification credential for
paper.summary: The compiler cRTL[KVAC, PRF] turns a keyed-verification credential (predicates ⊇ {φ_prf, φ_range}) and a compatible PRF into a one-more unforgeable rate-limiting anonymous token.

paper.statement: Let ` > 0, and Φ ⊃ {φprf , φrange }. Consider a one-more unforgeable keyed-
paper.summary: Instantiates the rate-limiting token from a one-more unforgeable KVAC_AT ∈ {μCMZ_AT, μBBS_AT} with an explicit advantage bound.

paper.statement: Let ` > 0, and Φ ⊃ {φprf , φrange }. If KVAC be a keyed-verification credential for
paper.summary: Bounds the one-more unforgeability advantage of cRTL[KVAC, PRF] via reductions to KVAC extractability, anonymity, and PRF security (Theorem 8.2 analysis).

paper.statement: In the random oracle model, if q-DDHI holds over GrGen, then HashDY is pseudo-
paper.summary: In the random oracle model, if q-DDHI holds over the group generator, the Dodis–Yampolskiy-style HashDY is a pseudorandom function.

paper.statement: Let KVAC be a keyed-verification credential for n = 1 attributes, with re-randomizable
paper.summary: A re-randomizable single-attribute keyed-verification credential yields a secure keyed-verification pseudonym system.

paper.statement: Consider a one-more unforgeable keyed-verification credential KVACAT ∈ {µCMZAT , µBBSAT },
paper.summary: Instantiates the pseudonym system from a one-more unforgeable KVAC_AT ∈ {μCMZ_AT, μBBS_AT}.

paper.statement: Let KVAC be a keyed-verification credential for n = 1 attributes, with re-randomizable
paper.summary: Security reduction underpinning the pseudonym construction (Theorem 8.9 analysis).

paper.statement: A relation family RF is admissible if: for any p.p.t. adversary A, it is compu-
paper.summary: A relation family is admissible if no p.p.t. adversary can find a non-zero kernel vector of the relation's matrix (or of its distinct group generators).

paper.statement: The protocol Σ for an admissible relation R F is strongly simulation-extractable in
paper.summary: The Fiat–Shamir Σ-protocol for an admissible linear relation is strongly simulation-extractable (straight-line) in the algebraic group and random oracle models.
